rationale: The cited paper appears only as a single supporting reference for a broad motivation claim that managers may want segments to respond differently to marketing mixes. The citing paper does not use Brusco et al. (2002)'s bicriterion partitioning formulation, simulated annealing heuristic, data, or evaluation framework in its model development or empirical analysis. Its core contribution instead extends Kim, Fong, and DeSarbo (2012) with Bayesian managerial constraints, so this citation functions as background context rather than a substantive building block.
